---
title: "About"
---

## Overview

This website automatically aggregates publications from members of the
[UK & Ireland Physics Higher Education Research (PER-HE) community](https://per-he.org).
Rather than manually maintaining a list that quickly becomes outdated, we use
[ORCID](https://orcid.org) profiles to keep the publication list current.

## How it works

1. **Author list**: Researchers in the community share their ORCID identifier,
   which is added to a CSV file in this repository.
2. **Automated fetching**: A weekly GitHub Actions workflow runs a Python script
   that queries the ORCID public API for each author's publications.
3. **Deduplication**: Papers appearing on multiple authors' profiles
   (collaborations) are merged into a single entry using their DOI.
4. **PER classification**: Papers are automatically tagged as Physics Education
   Research based on the journal name and title keywords.
5. **Website generation**: Quarto renders the data into this searchable,
   browsable website, deployed automatically to GitHub Pages.

## Limitations

- Only works added to an author's ORCID profile will appear here.
- Metadata quality depends on how entries were added to ORCID.
- There is up to a 7-day delay between an ORCID update and this site updating.

## Getting listed

If you are a member of the UK & Ireland PER community and would like your
publications included, please contact us via [per-he.org](https://per-he.org)
with your ORCID identifier.

## Source code

The source code for this site is available on
[GitHub](https://github.com/nicolaslab/per-he-publications).
It is written in Python and Quarto, and runs entirely on free GitHub
infrastructure.

## Acknowledgements

This site was inspired by the
[MVLS Learning, Teaching and Scholarship Network publications page](https://mvls-lts.github.io/outputs/),
which demonstrated this approach for a different research community.
We are grateful to its authors for making their
[source code](https://github.com/mvls-lts/outputs) openly available.

---

*Data sourced from [ORCID](https://orcid.org)*
